files.raw_diff:
diff --git a/frontend/src/App.tsx b/frontend/src/App.tsx --- a/frontend/src/App.tsx +++ b/frontend/src/App.tsx @@ -25,2 +25,3 @@

QA Platform Dashboard

+

scenarios.title: Verify Status Indicator visibility on main dashboard
scenarios.description: Ensure the StatusIndicator component is correctly rendered within the App layout.
scenarios.expected_result: The StatusIndicator component is visible to the user upon landing on the App page.

scenarios.title: Confirm Status Indicator reflects 'Active' state
scenarios.description: Verify the indicator displays the correct text and visual cues for an active status.
preconditions: System status is set to active
scenarios.expected_result: The indicator shows 'Active' text representing the current system state.

scenarios.title: Handle missing status data gracefully
scenarios.description: Check component behavior when the status data is null or undefined.
preconditions: Mock API to return empty status object
scenarios.expected_result: The component displays a fallback state (e.g., 'Unknown') rather than crashing.

scenarios.title: Status Indicator accessibility compliance
scenarios.description: Verify that the status indicator uses appropriate ARIA roles for screen readers.

steps.assertion: Should have aria-live attribute for dynamic updates

scenarios.expected_result: Screen readers can identify and announce status changes via ARIA attributes.

scenarios.title: Visual stability with long status labels
scenarios.description: Verify the layout remains intact when the status text is unusually long.
preconditions: Mock API to return a very long status string
scenarios.expected_result: The StatusIndicator container handles text overflow without breaking the App header layout.

scenarios.title: Prevent XSS via Status Text injection
scenarios.description: Ensure that status strings containing script tags are properly escaped and not executed.
preconditions: Mock API to return malicious status string

steps.assertion: HTML should be rendered as literal text

scenarios.expected_result: The application escapes HTML entities and prevents script execution.

scenarios.title: Responsive behavior of Status Indicator
scenarios.description: Verify the indicator is visible and correctly positioned on mobile viewports.

steps.assertion: Indicator must be visible on mobile

scenarios.expected_result: The StatusIndicator scales or adjusts appropriately for mobile screens without being hidden.

scenarios.title: Real-time status update reflection
scenarios.description: Verify the UI updates automatically when the backend status changes without a page refresh.
scenarios.expected_result: The StatusIndicator dynamically reflects state changes pushed from the server.

generated_specs.content: import os import pytest from playwright.sync_api import Page, expect BASE_URL = os.environ.get("BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8000") # SCENARIO_ID: bae0d458-588f-48a5-b8f7-6a368fdea008 def test_status_indicator_is_visible_on_main_dashboard(page: Page): page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/") status_indicator = page.get_by_role("status") expect(status_indicator).to_be_visible() # SCENARIO_ID: 58432760-04bb-4e7b-8286-d3eb2a5a326f def test_status_indicator_reflects_active_state(page: Page): page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/") status_indicator = page.get_by_role("status") expect(status_indicator).to_have_text("Active") # SCENARIO_ID: 5501f7d7-f1cc-44f2-a50f-549546f7d949 def test_status_indicator_falls_back_to_unknown_on_missing_data(page: Page): # Mock API to return empty status object page.route("**/api/status", lambda route: route.fulfill(json={})) page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/") status_indicator = page.get_by_role("status") expect(status_indicator).to_have_text("Unknown") # SCENARIO_ID: 52ae1a6d-2517-40c9-8b30-5d06fe8be91d def test_status_indicator_has_aria_live_polite_attribute(page: Page): page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/") status_indicator = page.get_by_role("status") expect(status_indicator).to_have_attribute("aria-live", "polite") # SCENARIO_ID: 093ec286-c9ab-4ddf-93b5-0a2d4d9f29f0 def test_status_indicator_handles_long_status_labels(page: Page): long_status = "System Maintenance Mode Underway" page.route("**/api/status", lambda route: route.fulfill(json={"status": long_status})) page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/") status_indicator = page.get_by_role("status") expect(status_indicator).to_be_visible() expect(status_indicator).to_have_text(long_status) # SCENARIO_ID: 65bb2a5c-8776-4448-a0af-06e77d30962e def test_status_indicator_escapes_xss_injection_strings(page: Page): malicious_payload = "" # Register dialog handler to fail if an alert is triggered page.on("dialog", lambda dialog: pytest.fail(f"XSS Vulnerability: Alert triggered with message: {dialog.message}")) page.route("**/api/status", lambda route: route.fulfill(json={"status": malicious_payload})) page.goto(f"{BASE_URL}/") # Verify that the payload is rendered as literal text and not as HTML expect(page.get_by_text("
